title: Systems Not Goals

content.statement: Goals define direction; systems deliver them.
content.question: Which goal are you obsessing over while ignoring the system that would deliver it?
content.mental_model: A goal is a wish; a system is a machine.
content.business_lens: Revenue targets fail without sales processes.
sop: Take one goal you're currently chasing and write the daily system that would actually produce it.
Rate your System Quality and Consistency each 1-10 (see calculation, page 5).
Remove the goal from your daily view, track only whether you ran the system today.
Review outcome metrics monthly, not daily, to avoid overreacting to noise.
If the outcome isn't moving after 90 days of a well-run system, redesign the system rather than just pushing harder.
questions: Which goal are you obsessing over while ignoring the system that would deliver it?
If you couldn't check your outcome metric for 90 days, would you still know whether you're on track?
What's the daily system behind your biggest goal right now, and when did you last actually run it?
content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10: System Quality 6, Consistency 9. Score = 6 times 9 = 54 out of 100. Higher means a decent system run consistently is out-producing a perfect system run sporadically; a high System Quality score paired with low Consistency means you built something great and then abandoned it.
content.closing_statement: Build the system before you chase the goal.

caption:
Principle Nº 108: Systems Not Goals

Goals define direction; systems deliver them.

Ask yourself: Which goal are you obsessing over while ignoring the system that would deliver it?
